title: Uncaught `FileNotFoundError` in `grafana_version()` when pebble socket closes during pod termination
summary: grafana_version() in src/grafana.py is a TOCTOU race: it guards with self._container.can_connect() (line 92) then calls self._container.exec([...]).wait_output() (line 94) with no exception handling. If the pebble socket closes between the check and the exec (pod termination / SIGTERM), a FileNotFoundError propagates uncaught and puts the unit in error state during config-changed/_reconcile.
why_good_first_issue: Single self-contained method, exact file:line given, and the fix pattern already exists elsewhere in the same file (other methods catch FileNotFoundError/ConnectionError). No architectural knowledge needed; just wrap the exec/wait_output in try/except and return "" (matching the existing not-connected branch).
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/grafana-k8s-operator && sed -n "86,100p" src/grafana.py' ; observe can_connect() guard at line 92 immediately followed by unguarded exec(...).wait_output() at line 94. Compare with line 524 (`except (FileNotFoundError, ProtocolError, PathError)`) and imports at lines 25-31 which already import ConnectionError/APIError, confirming the codebase already handles these elsewhere.
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: src/grafana.py line 92 `if not self._container.can_connect(): return ""` then line 94 `version_output, _ = self._container.exec(["grafana-server", "-v"]).wait_output()` has NO surrounding try/except. Observed: a FileNotFoundError/ConnectionError raised by exec() after a successful can_connect() check is uncaught. Expected: it should be caught and return "" like the not-connected case. Live race (pod-termination SIGTERM between check and exec) cannot be triggered without mutating the cos model, but the missing guard is unambiguous in code.
fix_hint: Wrap the exec/wait_output call in try/except catching the pebble/OS errors already imported (ConnectionError, APIError) plus FileNotFoundError, returning "" on failure (same as the can_connect() False branch). Mirror the existing handling at src/grafana.py:524.
notes: Issue auto-filed by solutions-qa from log analysis; traceback in body points exactly at grafana.py:94 and charm.py _reconcile -> set_workload_version. Unlabelled in tracker but textbook good-first-issue.

title: Incorrect cosl version dependency in grafana_dashboard library
summary: lib/charms/grafana_k8s/v0/grafana_dashboard.py declares PYDEPS = ["cosl >= 0.0.50"] (line 222) but imports `from cosl.types import type_convert_stored` (line 192) and `from cosl import DashboardPath40UID, LZMABase64` (line 191). type_convert_stored / DashboardPath40UID are only available in newer cosl, so a consuming charm that pins an old cosl per PYDEPS will hit ImportError at import time.
why_good_first_issue: One-line metadata fix in a clearly-bounded library: bump the PYDEPS lower bound to the cosl version that actually provides cosl.types.type_convert_stored and cosl.DashboardPath40UID, then bump LIBPATCH. No logic changes.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/grafana-k8s-operator && grep -n "cosl\|PYDEPS\|type_convert_stored\|DashboardPath40UID\|LIBPATCH" lib/charms/grafana_k8s/v0/grafana_dashboard.py | head'
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: line 191 `from cosl import DashboardPath40UID, LZMABase64`, line 192 `from cosl.types import type_convert_stored`, but line 222 `PYDEPS = ["cosl >= 0.0.50"]`. Observed: declared dependency floor (0.0.50) is far below the cosl version that introduced cosl.types.type_convert_stored / DashboardPath40UID. A charm honouring PYDEPS with an old cosl gets ImportError. (Note: current floor 0.0.50 is even lower than the >=0.5.0 quoted in the issue body, so the mismatch is real and arguably worse than reported.) Expected: PYDEPS floor >= the cosl release providing those symbols.
fix_hint: Raise PYDEPS lower bound to the cosl release that first ships cosl.types.type_convert_stored and cosl.DashboardPath40UID (issue cites ~1.3.0; verify against cosl changelog), then bump LIBPATCH (line 220) since lib metadata changed.
notes: Library-internal fix; affects downstream charms that vendor this lib. Verify the precise cosl version against the cosl package before finalising the bound.
